objective: Objectif de stratégie de trading crypto: Sur le marché GPSUSDC dans le timeframe 4h, nous allons utiliser l'indicateur ROC et l'entraînement d'un modèle d'apprentissage automatique pour anticiper les tendances haut/bas. Nous intégrerons ensuite un filtre anti-faux-signaux (confirmation inverse partielle) avec une gestion du risque non lineaire (SL/TP adaptes a la volatilite). Pour notre entrée, nous allons utiliser le ROC avec un seuil de 0.1 pour détecter les tendances haut/bas. Pour nos conditions de sortie, nous souhaitons obtenir une croissance positive sur le marché à moins de cinq jours. La gestion du risque sera établie en fonction des volatilités horaires de la journée. Pour notre risk management, nous fixerons un SL à 8% et TP à 12%. En termes d'hypothèse testable et falsifiable, cette stratégie doit prouver qu'elle peut anticiper les tendances haut/bas du marché avec une gestion de risque appropriée.

iterations.hypothesis: Bollinger breakout with ADX trend filter reduces false signals in ranging markets

pre_reflection.text:
[Pre-reflection] The strategy is designed to utilize Bollinger Breakout with ADX trend filter, RSI and ATR indicators in the 4h GPSUSDC market. The entry long condition involves close prices being above bollinger upper band and rsi >50 while adx >25 are met. Exit occurs when close price crosses over bollinger middle or adx <20. However, potential weaknesses include reliance on Bollinger breakout which might result in false signals during ranging markets.
[Backup plan] 1) Improve the robustness of strategy by incorporating additional indicators for confirming trade entries 2) Adjust risk management settings to reduce stop out occurrences and increase the profitability margin 3) Test alternative strategies such as more sophisticated trend-following models or mean reversion techniques. If results are poor, we will focus on refining our entry and exit signals.

iterations.hypothesis: Bollinger breakout with ADX trend filter reduces false signals in ranging markets

pre_reflection.text:
[Pre-reflection] This strategy aims to anticipate market trends by using ROC, an indicator that detects trending markets and combining it with an ADX trend filter. It also includes an anti-fake signal (partial inverse confirmation) mechanism. The risk management is set based on volatility of the day. If results are poor or mediocre, a potential weakness could be the sensitivity to market conditions. Adjusting parameters such as ROC threshold or period length may improve performance. For alternative approaches, we can consider other trend detection indicators like Moving Average Convergence Divergence (MACD).
[Backup plan] If the strategy yields negative returns, I would start by investigating potential reasons for poor performance and adjust the parameters accordingly. This could involve tweaking the ROC threshold or period length to better fit the market conditions. Additionally, a thorough backtest of different combinations of indicators can be performed to isolate which ones are contributing negatively to the results.

iterations.hypothesis: Fallback contractuel: proposition générée automatiquement pour maintenir la progression quand la sortie LLM n'est pas exploitable.

pre_reflection.text:
[Pre-reflection] The presented strategy seems to be solid and comprehensive, incorporating multiple indicators for better prediction of market trends. It also has risk management measures in place to control losses. However, it is important to consider potential weaknesses such as overfitting due to the use of a large number of indicators or lack of robustness due to high leverage on ROC indicator. Furthermore, the choice of SL/TP and their adaptation based on volatilities might be suboptimal without further analysis.
[Backup plan] If results are poor (negative return), we could try adjusting our risk management measures by using a more conservative stop-loss or taking profit strategy that is not solely dependent on volatility. If results are mediocre (low Sharpe), we can explore other indicators to improve the model's performance, such as incorporating volume data into our analysis.

iterations.hypothesis: Fallback contractuel: proposition générée automatiquement pour maintenir la progression quand la sortie LLM n'est pas exploitable.
Using a combination of MACD and RSI helps identify trend reversals in the market

pre_reflection.text:
[Pre-reflection] This strategy aims to identify trend reversals in the market using MACD and RSI. It considers trends as identified by both moving average convergence divergence (MACD) and relative strength index (RSI). The entry is based on a combination of these indicators, with long trades being triggered when the fast line crosses above the slow line (MACD), and when the RSI value exceeds 80. An exit strategy is also in place using a signal inverse. So far, the results are promising but not conclusive yet as only a few iterations have been completed.
[Backup plan] In case of poor performance, one possible change to consider would be adjusting the threshold for RSI values or exploring other indicators such as Bollinger Bands or Fibonacci Retracements. If the strategy is showing mediocre returns with low Sharpe ratio, it may be beneficial to explore a different risk management approach like hedging trades or diversifying across multiple assets.

iterations.hypothesis: Fallback contractuel: proposition générée automatiquement pour maintenir la progression quand la sortie LLM n'est pas exploitable.

pre_reflection.text:
[Pre-reflection] This strategy uses ROC and machine learning to anticipate market trends. It includes an anti-fake signal filter and non-linear risk management, with SL/TP adjustments for volatility. Hypothesis is falsifiable as it aims to prove the ability of predicting market trends with proper risk management. Recent iterations have shown positive returns but low Sharpe ratio.
[Backup plan] 1) Limitations: ROC may not be sensitive enough; ML model might overfit data; high SL/TP could limit profits if trend continues. 2) If results are poor, consider backtesting with different timeframes or machine learning models. 3) Adjust parameters like ROC threshold and risk management to improve Sharpe ratio (e.g., reduce SL/TP values). 4) Try a different approach such as breakout strategy, mean reversion, or sentiment analysis.

iterations.hypothesis: Ajustement structurel basé sur le diagnostic précédent.

pre_reflection.text:
[Pre-reflection] This strategy uses ROC to anticipate trends and incorporates anti-fraud measures, such as an inverse partial confirmation filter. However, it is not optimized for other market conditions or timeframes. If results are poor (negative return), the following changes could be tested: adjusting the threshold of ROC, using a different trend indicator, expanding the timeframe, or implementing risk management features to limit drawdown. In case of mediocre returns (low Sharpe ratio) it may be beneficial to increase trade frequency and consider other market conditions.
[Backup plan] 1. Test additional indicators and timeframes for better adaptability2. Adjust ROC threshold3. Increase trade frequency4. Implement diversification strategies5. Explore risk management features that could limit drawdown during poor performance

pre_reflection.text:
[Pre-reflection] Iteration 8 is preparing for potential weaknesses of the strategy and backup plans. The main weakness identified so far is that the Sharpe ratio in iterations 6, 7 was low at 0.230, indicating the performance might not be satisfactory. In case results are poor (negative return), we will try to adjust parameters such as ROC threshold or risk management parameters like SL and TP. If the current strategy fails, we would consider alternative approaches using different indicators or machine learning models.
[Backup plan] If the Sharpe ratio is low in subsequent iterations, we may need to tweak our approach by experimenting with various combinations of technical indicators (e.g., RSI, EMA, and ATR) and risk management parameters such as SL/TP levels. Additionally, exploring other machine learning models could be considered if necessary.

iterations.hypothesis: The combination of ATR based stop-loss/take-profit with ROC and Keltner filter can predict trend direction in the cryptocurrency market.

pre_reflection.text:
[Pre-reflection] The strategy uses ATR based stop-loss/take profit along with ROC, MACD and Bollinger bands to predict trend direction in the cryptocurrency market. The use of Keltner filter is not clear from the strategy description.
[Backup plan] If results are poor (negative return), we could try adjusting our entry conditions or changing our risk management settings. If results are mediocre (low Sharpe), we could try adjusting our stop-loss and take profit levels, or trying a different set of indicators.

iterations.hypothesis: This strategy uses ATR as a filter for trend strength and Bollinger bands with RSI to identify potential buy/sell points, resulting in higher returns.

pre_reflection.text:
[Pre-reflection] The strategy appears to be executing trades based on Bollinger Bands, RSI and ATR. The use of ADX adds an additional layer for filtering the strength of trends. It is important to note that this backtest has not provided any results yet.
[Backup plan] If the performance of the current approach does not meet our expectations or if it incurs significant losses, we will consider adjusting the following parameters: entry long close crosses Bollinger Band Upper and RSI above 50 with ADX greater than 25; exit close crosses Bollinger Middle band or ADX less than 20. We may also want to try using different indicators for trend strength, such as moving averages or Average Directional Index (ADX). Another alternative is to use a combination of multiple strategies rather than relying on one single strategy.
